title: Weather & Conditions
description: Driving in rain, fog, snow, at night, and across railroad tracks.

scenarios.prompt: The road is wet from rain. What should you do?

choices.label: Slow down and keep more distance
Drive the same as on a dry day
Go faster to finish sooner
Brake hard often to test the road
Turn on your high beams

feedback.correct: Yes — wet roads are slippery. Slow down and leave extra space to stop.
feedback.same: Wet roads need extra care. Slow down and keep more distance. Try again.
feedback.fast: Going fast on wet roads can make you slide. Slow down. Try again.
feedback.brake: Hard braking on a wet road can make you skid. Just slow down gently. Try again.
feedback.bright: High beams don't help in rain. Slow down and keep more distance. Try again.
feedback.default: Slow down and keep extra distance on wet roads. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: It's foggy and hard to see. Which lights should you use?

choices.label: Low beams (regular headlights)
High beams (brights)
No lights at all
Hazard lights while driving

feedback.correct: Yes — use low beams in fog. High beams bounce off the fog and make it harder to see.
feedback.high: High beams reflect off fog and blind you. Use low beams. Try again.
feedback.none: You still need lights in fog — use low beams. Try again.
feedback.hazard: Don't drive with hazard lights on — they're for a stopped car. Use low beams. Try again.
feedback.default: Use low beams in fog. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: The railroad lights are flashing and the gate is coming down. What should you do?

choices.label: Stop and wait for the train to pass
Drive across quickly before the train
Drive around the gate
Honk and creep slowly across

feedback.correct: Yes — stop and wait. Never try to beat a train or go around the gate.
feedback.race: Never try to beat a train — stop and wait. Try again.
feedback.around: Never drive around a lowered gate. Stop and wait for the train. Try again.
feedback.creep: Don't move onto the tracks at all — stop and wait for the train. Try again.
feedback.default: Stop and wait for the train. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: Your car starts to skid. What should you do?

choices.label: Ease off the gas and steer where you want to go
Brake hard and hold the wheel still
Speed up to drive out of it
Turn the wheel sharply the other way

feedback.correct: Yes — take your foot off the gas and gently steer where you want to go. Don't slam the brakes.
feedback.brakehard: Braking hard can make the skid worse. Ease off the gas and steer where you want to go. Try again.
feedback.faster: Speeding up makes a skid worse. Ease off the gas and steer where you want to go. Try again.
feedback.yank: A sharp jerk can spin you. Gently steer where you want to go and ease off the gas. Try again.
feedback.default: Ease off the gas and steer where you want to go. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: On a very wet road your tires start to ride up on the water and the steering feels light. What should you do?

choices.label: Ease off the gas until the tires grip again
Brake hard right away
Speed up to push through the water
Turn the wheel quickly side to side

feedback.correct: Yes — take your foot off the gas and hold the wheel steady until the tires touch the road again. Don't brake hard.
feedback.brake: Hard braking while hydroplaning can make you skid. Ease off the gas instead. Try again.
feedback.faster: Going faster keeps you up on the water. Ease off the gas. Try again.
feedback.turn: Quick turns can spin you on the water. Hold the wheel steady and ease off the gas. Try again.
feedback.default: Ease off the gas and hold the wheel steady. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: It is near freezing and the road looks shiny and wet. It may be icy. What should you do?

choices.label: Slow down and avoid sudden braking or turning
Drive the same as on a dry day
Brake hard to test how slick it is
Turn on your high beams

feedback.correct: Yes — slow down and make gentle moves. Ice can be invisible, and bridges freeze first.
feedback.same: Ice is far more slippery than a wet road. Slow down and be gentle. Try again.
feedback.test: Braking hard on ice makes you skid. Just slow down gently. Try again.
feedback.bright: High beams don't help on ice. Slow down and make gentle moves. Try again.
feedback.default: Slow down and avoid sudden braking or turning on ice. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: A tire suddenly blows out and the car pulls to one side. What should you do?

choices.label: Hold the wheel firmly, ease off the gas, and slow down gently
Slam on the brakes right away
Steer sharply onto the shoulder
Speed up to keep control

feedback.correct: Yes — grip the wheel, take your foot off the gas, and let the car slow before easing off the road.
feedback.brake: Hard braking after a blowout can spin you. Ease off the gas and slow down gently. Try again.
feedback.yank: A sharp turn can make you lose control. Hold the wheel steady and slow down gently. Try again.
feedback.faster: Speeding up is dangerous with a blown tire. Ease off the gas and slow down gently. Try again.
feedback.default: Hold the wheel firmly, ease off the gas, and slow down gently. Try again.

scenarios.prompt: At night, a car coming toward you has bright high beams and it's hard to see. What should you do?

choices.label: Look toward the right edge of your lane until they pass
Look right at their headlights
Turn on your own high beams to get even
Close your eyes for a second

feedback.correct: Yes — look toward the right edge of your lane so the bright lights don't blind you, and keep driving slowly.
feedback.stare: Looking right at the lights blinds you. Look toward the right edge of your lane. Try again.
feedback.bright: High beams back will blind you both. Look toward the right edge of your lane. Try again.
feedback.close: Never close your eyes while driving. Look toward the right edge of your lane. Try again.
feedback.default: Look toward the right edge of your lane until the car passes. Try again.
